workflow.name: Screen CVs for multiple roles with OpenRouter, Google Sheets, Gmail and Calendly
workflow.description:
This template automates the complete hiring pipeline for 
digital agencies managing applications across multiple job 
roles. When a candidate submits a Google Form with their 
CV, the system scores it with AI against a role-specific 
weighted job profile, lets HR decide in Google Sheets with 
a single typed decision, sends a personalized email 
automatically, and tracks the Calendly interview booking 
in real time — all without leaving the tools you already use.

This template contains 3 linked workflows that must all 
be active for the full pipeline to run.

## How it works

**Workflow 1 — CV Screening Pipeline**

- Triggers when a new row is added to the Google Form 
  responses sheet
- Extracts the Google Drive file ID from the CV upload 
  URL, handling all three Drive URL formats automatically
- Checks an n8n Data Table to block duplicate 
  applications by email — stops processing if the 
  candidate already exists
- Routes the candidate to the correct job profile based 
  on their selected role using a Switch node
- Each job profile (Set node) defines: required skills, 
  nice-to-have skills, minimum years of experience, 
  seniority thresholds (Junior / Mid / Senior), and 
  weighted scoring criteria (technical skills weight, 
  experience weight, nice-to-have weight)
- All branches converge into a single Normalize Data 
  node that cleans Google Form field names into 
  standardized keys
- CV is downloaded from Google Drive and extracted from 
  PDF to plain text
- AI evaluates the CV using a dynamically built prompt 
  pulling all values from the job profile — no 
  hardcoded role logic in the AI node
- Structured output parser returns: score (out of 10), 
  seniority level, recommendation (Interview / Maybe / 
  Reject), matched skills, missing skills, 
  nice-to-have matched, experience comment, red flags, 
  and a plain English summary
- All results are written back to the same Google Sheet 
  row matched by the candidate's email address

**Workflow 2 — HR Decision Email Sender**

- Watches the Google Sheet every minute for updates to 
  the HR_Decision column
- An IF node confirms the decision is filled and 
  Email_Status is not already "Sent" — preventing 
  duplicate emails if the sheet is updated again
- HR types exactly "Send Invite" or "Send Rejection" 
  in the HR_Decision column to trigger the correct path
- Interview invitation email includes a pre-filled 
  Calendly booking link with the candidate's name and 
  email already populated as URL parameters
- Rejection email delivers a warm, personalized message 
  referencing the role applied for
- After sending, Email_Status is immediately updated 
  to "Sent" in the sheet

**Workflow 3 — Calendly Interview Tracker**

- Listens for Calendly webhook events in real time: 
  invitee.created and invitee.canceled
- On booking: extracts the interview start time, 
  formats it to your local timezone, and writes it to 
  the Interview_Time column matched by email
- On cancellation: checks the rescheduled flag — if 
  true, stops and lets the new invitee.created event 
  handle the update; if false, writes "Meeting 
  canceled" to the sheet

## How to use

1. Connect Google Sheets Trigger credential (OAuth2) 
   and point it to your Form Responses sheet
2. Connect Google Drive credential (OAuth2) — needs 
   read access to the folder where Google Forms 
   stores CV uploads
3. Connect OpenRouter credential — get a free API key 
   at openrouter.ai. Default model is set to a free 
   model for testing. Switch to a stronger model for 
   production
4. Connect Gmail credential (OAuth2) for sending 
   invitation and rejection emails
5. Connect Calendly credential (OAuth2)
6. In the **Invitation Message** node, replace 
   YOUR_CALENDLY_LINK with your actual Calendly 
   event URL
7. Add these columns manually to your Google Form 
   responses sheet: score, seniority, recommendation, 
   matched_skills, missing_skills, nice_to_have_matched, 
   experience_comment, red_flags, summary, HR_Decision, 
   Email_Status, Interview_Time
8. Create an n8n Data Table named **CV Screening** 
   with a single column: Email (string type)
9. Customize each Job Profile Set node with your 
   agency's actual role requirements, skill lists, 
   and scoring weights
10. Activate all three workflows

## Requirements

- **Google Sheets** (OAuth2) — application tracking 
  and HR decision sheet
- **Google Drive** (OAuth2) — CV file storage and 
  download
- **Gmail** (OAuth2) — sending invitation and 
  rejection emails
- **OpenRouter** (API key) — AI model access for 
  CV evaluation
- **Calendly** (OAuth2) — interview booking and 
  cancellation tracking
- **n8n Data Table** — built-in n8n feature, 
  no external service needed

## Notes & customization

- **To add a new job role:** add the role to your 
  Google Form dropdown, add a new condition to the 
  Route by Job Role Switch node, and duplicate any 
  existing Job Profile Set node with updated values
- **Scoring weights:** tech_weight, experience_weight, 
  and nice_to_have_weight in each Job Profile node 
  must add up to 100
- **HR_Decision values are case-sensitive:** type 
  Send Invite or Send Rejection exactly as written
- **Calendly email matching:** the candidate must 
  book using the same email they applied with — the 
  invitation link pre-fills this automatically so 
  this is handled in normal use
- **Free model limits:** OpenRouter free models 
  allow approximately 200 requests per day, which 
  is sufficient for most agency hiring volumes
---

## 📬 Contact Information
For Help and queries, contact

* **LinkedIn:** [Salman Mehboob](https://www.linkedin.com/in/salman-mehboob-pro/)
* **Email:**[salmanmehboob1947@gmail.com](mailto:salmanmehboob1947@gmail.com)

assignments.name: cover_note
assignments.value: ={{ $('Extract CV File ID').item.json['Anything you want us to know? (optional)  '] }}

messageValues.message:
=You are a senior recruitment analyst at a digital staffing agency. You evaluate candidate CVs against specific job profiles and produce objective, evidence-based assessments that hiring managers use to make interview decisions.

EVALUATION RULES:

1. SCORING METHOD (score out of 10):
You must calculate the score using a weighted formula:
- Technical Skills Score × {{ $('Normalize Data').item.json.tech_weight }}%
- Experience Score × {{ $('Normalize Data').item.json.experience_weight }}%
- Nice-to-Have Skills Score × {{ $('Normalize Data').item.json.nice_to_have_weight }}%

Score each component from 0–10 before applying the weight. Combine for the final score. Round to one decimal place.

2. SENIORITY DETERMINATION:
Assign seniority based on the following thresholds for this role:
- Junior: {{ $('Normalize Data').item.json.junior_threshold }}
- Mid: {{ $('Normalize Data').item.json.mid_threshold }}
- Senior: {{ $('Normalize Data').item.json.senior_threshold }}

Always cross-check the candidate's declared years against actual CV evidence. If the CV shows less real experience than declared, downgrade the seniority. If the CV shows stronger evidence than declared years suggest, you may upgrade.

3. RECOMMENDATION LOGIC:
- Interview → final score 7.0 or above AND no critical required skills missing
- Maybe → final score 5.0 to 6.9 OR one or two required skills missing but strong in others
- Reject → final score below 5.0 OR more than half of required skills absent from CV

4. RED FLAG DETECTION:
Flag any of the following if present:
- Required skills listed by candidate but no project or employer evidence found in CV
- Years of experience declared significantly exceeds what CV timeline supports
- CV contains no measurable outcomes, no tool names, no real project detail
- Employment gaps longer than 6 months with no explanation
- CV is clearly generic or template-filled with no role-specific content

5. OUTPUT RULES:
- Return ONLY the JSON object. No text before it. No text after it. No markdown fences.
- matched_skills must only list items from the required skills list that appear with evidence in the CV
- missing_skills must only list required skills not evidenced in the CV
- nice_to_have_matched must only list items from the nice-to-have list found in the CV
- red_flags must be "None" if no flags found
- All values must be plain strings — no nested objects, no arrays, no markdown

Output Should be:

{
  "score": "string",
  "seniority": "string",
  "recommendation": "string",
  "matched_skills": "string",
  "missing_skills": "string",
  "nice_to_have_matched": "string",
  "experience_comment": "string",
  "red_flags": "string",
  "summary": "string"
}


Return only the raw JSON object. No markdown. No backticks. No explanation before or after.

parameters.content:
## 📥 Application Entry

Triggers every minute when a new row is added to the 
Google Form responses sheet. Extracts the Google Drive 
file ID from the CV upload URL — handles all 3 Drive 
URL formats automatically.

## 🛡️ Duplicate Guard

Checks the n8n internal Data Table to see if this 
email has already been processed. If the email exists, 
the workflow stops — preventing duplicate evaluations. 
If new, the email is logged to the table before 
continuing.

## ⚙️ Role-Based Job Profiles

Routes the candidate to the correct job profile based 
on the role they selected in the form. Each profile 
defines the required skills, nice-to-have skills, 
minimum experience, seniority thresholds, and weighted 
scoring criteria for that specific role.

⚠️→ Customize each Job Profile node to match your 
  agency's hiring requirements.

## 🗂️ Normalize Candidate Data

Merges all 4 job profile branches into a single clean 
data object. Standardizes messy Google Form field names 
(with trailing spaces) into clean keys used by all 
downstream nodes.

## 📄 CV Extraction

Downloads the candidate's CV from Google Drive using 
the extracted file ID. Converts the PDF into plain 
text so the AI model can read and evaluate it.

## 📊 Results Logged to Sheet

Writes all AI evaluation results back to the same 
Google Form responses sheet, matched by email address. 
HR can now see score, seniority, and recommendation 
directly alongside the original application row.

## 🤖 AI Evaluation Engine

Sends the CV text and job profile to the AI model via 
a dynamically built prompt. The system prompt and 
scoring weights are pulled from the job profile — no 
hardcoding. Returns a structured JSON with:

score · seniority · recommendation · matched_skills  
missing_skills · nice_to_have_matched · red_flags · summary

→ Change the model in OpenRouter Chat Model node.
  Default: openrouter/free (for testing).
  Switch to gpt-4o or deepseek for production.

assignments.name: message
assignments.value:
=Hi {{ $json['Full Name  '] }},

Thank you for applying for the {{ $json['Role Applying For  '] }} position. We reviewed your CV and were very impressed with your background. We would love to invite you to an interview!

Please select a time that works best for you using the link below. For your convenience, your name and email are already pre-filled:

your-calandly-link-here?email={{ $json['Email Address'] }}&name={{ $json['Full Name  '] }}

We look forward to speaking with you soon!

Best regards,
The Hiring Team

assignments.name: message
assignments.value:
=Hi {{ $json['Full Name  '] }},

Thank you so much for taking the time to share your CV with us for the {{ $json['Role Applying For  '] }} position. We truly appreciate the effort you put into your application.

After careful review, we have decided to move forward with other candidates whose profiles more closely match our exact project needs at this moment. Please know that this was a difficult decision, as we saw some great qualities and potential in your background!

The tech and digital space is incredibly vast, and your passion for this field is clear. We highly encourage you to keep building your skills, working on exciting new projects, and pushing your boundaries. Every application is just a stepping stone leading you to the perfect opportunity. 

Keep up the great work, and don't stop building! We wish you nothing but the absolute best in your career journey and hope our paths might cross again in the future.

Warmly,
The Hiring Team

assignments.name: subject
assignments.value: =Update on your application for {{ $json['Role Applying For  '] }}

parameters.content:
## 👁️ HR Decision Watcher

Polls the sheet every minute watching for changes to 
the HR_Decision column. The IF node ensures the email 
only sends when HR_Decision is filled AND Email_Status 
is not already "Sent" — preventing any duplicate 
emails.

→ HR must type exactly:
  "Send Invite" — to invite the candidate
  "Send Rejection" — to reject the candidate

## ✉️ Email Template Builder

Routes to the correct email template based on the HR 
decision. The invitation email includes a pre-filled 
Calendly link with the candidate's name and email. 
The rejection email delivers a respectful, encouraging 
message.

⚠️→ Replace YOUR_CALENDLY_LINK in the Invitation 
  Message node with your actual Calendly URL.

## 📬 Send & Confirm

Sends the email via Gmail and immediately writes 
"Sent" to the Email_Status column in the sheet. 
This status flag prevents the workflow from sending 
the same email twice if the sheet is updated again.

parameters.content:
## 📅 Calendly Event Listener

Listens for two Calendly events in real time:
- invitee.created — candidate booked an interview
- invitee.canceled — candidate canceled

Routes each event to the correct branch for handling.

## ✅ Interview Booked

When a candidate books an interview, the start time 
is extracted from the Calendly payload, formatted 
to your local timezone, and written to the 
Interview_Time column in the sheet — matched by 
the candidate's email address.

→ Change the timezone format in the Date & Time 
  node to match your region.

## ❌ Cancellation Handler

When a candidate cancels, the IF node checks if 
this is a true cancellation or a reschedule. If 
rescheduled (rescheduled = true), the workflow 
stops — the new booking event handles the update 
automatically. If genuinely canceled, "Meeting 
canceled" is written to the Interview_Time column.

## 🏢 AI CV Screening & Interview Pipeline for Agencies

**Built for agencies hiring across multiple roles.**  
A candidate fills a Google Form → AI scores their CV → 
HR decides in a sheet → email sends automatically → 
Calendly tracks the interview. Fully connected. Zero manual steps.

---

### 👤 Who Is This For?
Digital agencies and hiring teams managing applications 
for multiple job roles simultaneously. Works out of the 
box for: n8n Automation Developer, React Developer, 
SEO Expert, Meta Ads Expert. Swap in any roles you need.

---

### ⚙️ How It Works

**WORKFLOW 1 — CV Screening Pipeline**

1. Triggers every minute when a new Google Form 
   response row is added to the sheet
2. Extracts the Google Drive file ID from the CV 
   upload URL (handles all 3 Drive URL formats)
3. Checks n8n Data Table — if email already exists, 
   workflow stops (duplicate protection)
4. New email is logged to the Data Table immediately
5. Routes candidate to the correct job profile based 
   on the role they selected in the form
6. Each job profile defines: required skills, 
   nice-to-have skills, minimum years, seniority 
   thresholds (Junior/Mid/Senior), and scoring 
   weights (technical/experience/nice-to-have)
7. All branches merge into one clean data object 
   with standardized field names
8. CV is downloaded from Google Drive using the 
   extracted file ID
9. PDF is converted to plain text
10. AI evaluates the CV against the job profile using 
    a dynamically built prompt — scoring is weighted 
    per role, not generic
11. Returns structured JSON: score, seniority, 
    recommendation, matched skills, missing skills, 
    nice-to-have matched, red flags, summary
12. All results written back to the same Google 
    Sheet row, matched by email address

**WORKFLOW 2 — HR Decision Email Sender**

13. Watches the sheet every minute for changes to 
    the HR_Decision column
14. IF node checks: HR_Decision is filled AND 
    Email_Status is not already "Sent" (prevents 
    duplicate emails)
15. HR types exactly "Send Invite" or 
    "Send Rejection" in the HR_Decision column
16. Switch routes to the correct email template
17. Invite email includes a pre-filled Calendly 
    link with candidate name and email
18. Rejection email sends a respectful, 
    encouraging message
19. Gmail sends the email
20. Email_Status column is updated to "Sent" 
    immediately after sending

**WORKFLOW 3 — Calendly Interview Tracker**

21. Listens for Calendly events in real time: 
    invitee.created and invitee.canceled
22. Routes by event type
23. Booking: extracts start time, formats to 
    local timezone, writes to Interview_Time column
24. Cancellation: checks if this is a real 
    cancellation or a reschedule — if rescheduled, 
    stops (the new booking event handles it); 
    if genuinely canceled, writes "Meeting canceled"

---

### 🛠️ Setup Steps

- [ ] Connect Google Sheets Trigger credential  
      (use OAuth2, point to your Form Responses sheet)
- [ ] Connect Google Drive credential  
      (needs read access to the Drive folder where  
      Google Form uploads CVs)
- [ ] Connect OpenRouter credential  
      (get free API key at openrouter.ai — free models  
      available for testing)
- [ ] Connect Gmail credential (OAuth2)
- [ ] Connect Calendly credential (OAuth2)
- [ ] In **Invitation Message** node → replace  
      YOUR_CALENDLY_LINK with your actual Calendly URL
- [ ] Add these columns to your Google Sheet manually:  
      score | seniority | recommendation |  
      matched_skills | missing_skills |  
      nice_to_have_matched | experience_comment |  
      red_flags | summary | HR_Decision |  
      Email_Status | Interview_Time
- [ ] Create an n8n Data Table named "CV Screening"  
      with one column: Email (string)
- [ ] Customize each Job Profile Set node with your  
      agency's actual role requirements and skill lists

---

### 🎨 Customization

**To add a new job role:**
1. Add a new option to your Google Form dropdown
2. Add a new condition to the Route by Job Role 
   Switch node
3. Duplicate any Job Profile Set node and update 
   the skill fields for the new role


**To change scoring weights:**
Open the relevant Job Profile Set node and update 
tech_weight, experience_weight, nice_to_have_weight. 
They must add up to 100.

---

### ⚠️ Important Notes

- HR_Decision column values are case-sensitive:  
  type **Send Invite** or **Send Rejection** exactly
- The Calendly booking email must match the  
  candidate's application email for Interview_Time  
  to update correctly (the invite link pre-fills  
  this automatically)
- This template contains 3 separate workflows —  
  all 3 must be active for the full pipeline to work

user.bio: n8n Automation Engineer with 3+ years building real-world workflows for agencies and businesses. I specialize in AI pipelines, voice automation with Vapi, lead scraping, outreach systems and API integrations. I build things that actually work in production not just demos.

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.
